summary: I was born and raised in Nigeria, where life was challenging but somewhat normal since I had access to some social benefits. The high crime rate made living peacefully difficult, and the cost of education was very expensive. These harsh living conditions motivated me to leave Nigeria and start a new life elsewhere.

Specifically, I grew up in Port Harcourt, Nigeria, which was my hometown and where I experienced the difficulties of daily life. Despite the challenges, I had family and friends there, including my aunts, uncles, cousins, and my grandpa. Leaving Port Harcourt was hard because it meant leaving behind my childhood and many loved ones.

I moved to the United States in June 2019 to live with my dad and brother, seeking better living conditions and more affordable education. Life in the U.S. is very different from Nigeria; for example, walking on the streets feels much safer, and going to school is more enjoyable. Although I miss my mom and the life I left behind, I hope to adapt and build a new life here.
